$description: Source-neutral concept catalog. Each entry maps a snake_case canonical concept name to the literal contest-title patterns that identify it in each source's files, plus a `caveats` field spelling out what is and is NOT comparable across sources. Use scripts/concepts.py:concept_for(contest, source) for row-by-row lookup.

concepts.name: presidential_electors
concepts.description: Electors for President and Vice President of the United States.
concepts.caveats: Boulder presents the elector slate as a single candidate (`Joseph R. Biden / Kamala D. Harris`); SOS lists the presidential candidate alone (`Joseph R. Biden`). Vote totals match exactly when aggregated to the candidate level, but the candidate strings cannot be joined directly — strip the running-mate suffix from Boulder rows before joining.

concepts.name: us_representative
concepts.description: United States Representative — Boulder is mostly in CO-2, with a small piece historically in CO-4.
concepts.caveats: District numbers (`113th Congress`, `114th Congress`, etc.) change by election; group on this concept to avoid having to enumerate every Congress. District boundaries also shift after each census (2010, 2020) so historical comparisons across boundary years are geographic-area comparisons, not constituency comparisons.

concepts.name: governor
concepts.caveats: Like president, Boulder presents the ticket as one candidate (`Polis/Primavera`); SOS varies. Aggregate to candidate level after splitting the slash.

concepts.name: state_senator
concepts.caveats: District boundaries are redrawn after each census. Cross-decennial comparisons need a precinct-to-district crosswalk for each year.

concepts.name: state_representative
concepts.caveats: Same caveat as state senator on redistricting.

concepts.name: regent_university_of_colorado
concepts.description: Regent of the University of Colorado.

concepts.name: district_attorney
concepts.description: Boulder County District Attorney (20th Judicial District).

concepts.name: county_commissioner
concepts.caveats: SOS does NOT publish precinct-level county-commissioner results — the Boulder County file is the only source. Reconciliation cross-checks therefore only work BoCo→BoCo for this concept.

concepts.name: city_of_boulder_council
concepts.caveats: City-level office; only published in Boulder County files. Starting in 2023, the Boulder Mayor race became RCV — see `city_of_boulder_mayor` for that. Council remains plurality.

concepts.name: city_of_boulder_mayor
concepts.caveats: First-ever RCV election in 2023. RCV rows in the harmonized schema use `contest_type = 'ranked_choice'` and suffix `candidate_or_option` with `| Round N` (intermediate rounds, may have NEGATIVE votes for ballots transferring away) or `| Final` (the candidate's final round total). Sum only `| Final` rows to get totals comparable to a plurality race.

concepts.name: city_of_longmont_council
concepts.caveats: City-level office; only Boulder County files.

concepts.name: city_of_louisville_council
concepts.caveats: City-level office; only Boulder County files.

concepts.name: city_of_lafayette_council
concepts.caveats: City-level office; only Boulder County files.
